# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.106.0/http/server.ts";

const DEFAULT_MODULES = ["https://example.com/defaultModule.ts"];

async function main(): Promise<void> {
  console.log("Starting server...");

  const server = serve({ port: 8000 });

  for await (const req of server) {
    const queryParams = new URLSearchParams(req.url.split('?')[1]);
    const modulePaths = queryParams.get('modulePaths')?.split(',') || DEFAULT_MODULES;

    let loadedModules = [];
    for (let path of modulePaths) {
      try {
        const module = await import(path);
        loadedModules.push(module);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error(`Failed to load module at ${path}:`, err)
      }
    }

    // Use loadedModules here...

    req.respond({ body: "Hello, world!\n" });
  }
}

main();
